verses.text: As Ezra prays and weeps before the house of God, a great weeping assembly gathers, and Shechaniah says there is yet hope: let them covenant to put away the foreign wives. 'Arise... we also will be with thee.' Conviction turns to resolve. Sorrow for sin must move on to decisive amendment.

As Ezra prays and weeps before the house of God, a great weeping assembly gathers, and Shechaniah says there is yet hope: let them covenant to put away the foreign wives. 'Arise... we also will be with thee.' Conviction turns to resolve. Sorrow for sin must move on to decisive amendment.

As Ezra prays and weeps before the house of God, a great weeping assembly gathers, and Shechaniah says there is yet hope: let them covenant to put away the foreign wives. 'Arise... we also will be with thee.' Conviction turns to resolve. Sorrow for sin must move on to decisive amendment.

As Ezra prays and weeps before the house of God, a great weeping assembly gathers, and Shechaniah says there is yet hope: let them covenant to put away the foreign wives. 'Arise... we also will be with thee.' Conviction turns to resolve. Sorrow for sin must move on to decisive amendment.

Ezra makes the leaders swear to do this, then withdraws to mourn, eating no bread. His grief does not lift the moment a plan is made. Deep repentance keeps fasting until the wrong is actually set right.

Ezra makes the leaders swear to do this, then withdraws to mourn, eating no bread. His grief does not lift the moment a plan is made. Deep repentance keeps fasting until the wrong is actually set right.

A proclamation summons all the returned exiles to Jerusalem within three days, on pain of forfeiting goods and being cut off. The matter is treated with urgent seriousness. Sin that has gripped a whole community calls for clear and resolute action.

A proclamation summons all the returned exiles to Jerusalem within three days, on pain of forfeiting goods and being cut off. The matter is treated with urgent seriousness. Sin that has gripped a whole community calls for clear and resolute action.

All the men gather, trembling in heavy rain, and Ezra charges them, 'make confession... and do his pleasure: and separate yourselves.' Confession must be joined to separation. To own sin yet cling to it is no repentance at all.

All the men gather, trembling in heavy rain, and Ezra charges them, 'make confession... and do his pleasure: and separate yourselves.' Confession must be joined to separation. To own sin yet cling to it is no repentance at all.

All the men gather, trembling in heavy rain, and Ezra charges them, 'make confession... and do his pleasure: and separate yourselves.' Confession must be joined to separation. To own sin yet cling to it is no repentance at all.

The assembly agrees but, because of the rain and the numbers, asks that the matter be handled in order over time. They embrace the duty while seeking to do it wisely. Zeal and good order are not enemies; great tasks need both resolve and method.

The assembly agrees but, because of the rain and the numbers, asks that the matter be handled in order over time. They embrace the duty while seeking to do it wisely. Zeal and good order are not enemies; great tasks need both resolve and method.

The assembly agrees but, because of the rain and the numbers, asks that the matter be handled in order over time. They embrace the duty while seeking to do it wisely. Zeal and good order are not enemies; great tasks need both resolve and method.

A few oppose the plan, but the rest proceed, examining the cases family by family over three months. The painful work is done thoroughly, not hastily. Setting right a widespread sin takes patient, honest labour, case by case.

A few oppose the plan, but the rest proceed, examining the cases family by family over three months. The painful work is done thoroughly, not hastily. Setting right a widespread sin takes patient, honest labour, case by case.

A few oppose the plan, but the rest proceed, examining the cases family by family over three months. The painful work is done thoroughly, not hastily. Setting right a widespread sin takes patient, honest labour, case by case.

Even among the priests, men are found with foreign wives, and they pledge their hand to put them away and bring a guilt offering. The leaders are not spared the reckoning. Those nearest the altar must be first to judge their own sin.

Even among the priests, men are found with foreign wives, and they pledge their hand to put them away and bring a guilt offering. The leaders are not spared the reckoning. Those nearest the altar must be first to judge their own sin.

Even among the priests, men are found with foreign wives, and they pledge their hand to put them away and bring a guilt offering. The leaders are not spared the reckoning. Those nearest the altar must be first to judge their own sin.

The Levites, singers, gatekeepers, and many of the people are likewise named as having married foreign wives, and they put them away. The roll of the guilty is recorded without flinching. God's people deal honestly with sin in their own ranks, however costly and however high it reaches.
